description: Builds and maintains pipelines that connect large language models and AI APIs into existing web applications and backend systems. Designs prompt workflows, manages API orchestration, and ensures reliable AI feature delivery in production environments.
stat_text: Job postings requiring generative AI skills grew by over 1,000% between 2022 and 2024, with AI integration and deployment roles among the fastest-growing technical positions.
stat_title: Future of Work Report: AI at Work
fit: Your existing skills in APIs, backend integration, and building web systems transfer directly, but you must shift from consuming APIs to orchestrating AI model behavior and managing unpredictable outputs in production.
steps_json: ["Complete DeepLearning.AI's LangChain short course on Udemy", "Build a working chatbot using OpenAI API and LangChain in a personal project", "Deploy a Hugging Face model via API into an existing web app you own"]

description: Architects the information environment that AI models work within — designing system prompts, retrieval pipelines, memory structures, and context windows that determine what a model sees and how it responds. Distinct from prompt engineering in that the focus is on the full context architecture, not individual prompts.
stat_text: Researchers and practitioners are converging on "context engineering" as the core skill in AI product development — the discipline of precisely controlling what information models receive to produce reliable outputs.
stat_title: From vibe coding to context engineering: 2025 in software development
fit: Web developers already structure information for browsers and APIs — the same instinct applies here, shifted to structuring what AI models see. The job requires understanding retrieval, token budgets, and how context shapes model behavior.
steps_json: ["Complete fast.ai or DeepLearning.AI RAG course to learn retrieval-augmented generation", "Build a production context pipeline using LangChain and LlamaIndex with multiple data sources", "Read and implement the techniques from Anthropic's prompt engineering guide end-to-end"]

description: Bridges the gap between product management and engineering by building full-stack web features powered by AI models, owning everything from UI to model selection and evaluation. Takes AI prototypes from proof-of-concept to scalable, user-facing products.
stat_text: 76% of developers expect to use or are already using AI tools in their development process, with the majority integrating them directly into their coding workflow.
fit: Your full-stack UI and API skills transfer directly, but you must shift from displaying data to designing around unpredictable model outputs and evaluation loops.
steps_json: ["Build a chatbot using Vercel AI SDK and OpenAI API with streaming responses", "Complete Supabase's official full-stack Next.js tutorial, adding pgvector for semantic search", "Ship a small public tool that lets users rate AI outputs, so you practice prompt iteration and evaluation"]

description: Works at the intersection of Figma and production code, translating visual designs into polished, accessible React components and design systems. Owns the implementation layer between design and engineering: building component libraries, interaction patterns, and the pixel-level details that make products feel right.
stat_text: Figma's 2024 design-to-code report found that design-to-development handoff is the single biggest source of friction in product teams, with 60% of developers citing misaligned specs as a top time drain — the problem Design Engineers are hired to solve.
stat_title: Bridging the gap between design and code
fit: Web development's HTML, CSS, and React skills carry over directly, but the job shifts from building features to perfecting the craft of UI implementation — owning component fidelity, interaction quality, and design system coherence rather than feature delivery.
steps_json: ["Build and publish a React component library to npm with a Storybook docs site", "Learn Framer Motion: rebuild one polished animation from a site you admire", "Contribute to an open-source design system (shadcn/ui, Radix, MUI)"]

description: Designs and implements pipelines that connect large language models and AI APIs into existing enterprise software systems, ensuring reliability, security, and scalability. Day-to-day work involves prompt engineering, API orchestration, and building middleware layers between AI services and business applications.
core_tools: LangChain, OpenAI API, Azure OpenAI Service
stat_text: Job postings requiring generative AI skills grew by over 1,000% between 2022 and 2024, with AI integration and deployment roles among the fastest-growing technical positions.
stat_title: Future of Work Report: AI at Work
fit: Your coding and systems-thinking skills transfer directly, but you must shift from writing deterministic logic to orchestrating unpredictable, probabilistic AI outputs.
steps_json: ["Complete DeepLearning.AI's LangChain for LLM Application Development course", "Build a working middleware project connecting OpenAI API to a mock enterprise database", "Earn the Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate (AI-102) certification"]

description: Builds and maintains the infrastructure and CI/CD pipelines required to deploy, monitor, and retrain machine learning models in production environments. Responsibilities include model versioning, performance monitoring, drift detection, and automating model lifecycle management.
stat_text: The global MLOps market is projected to grow from $1.7 billion in 2022 to $13.0 billion by 2027, representing a compound annual growth rate of 39.7%.
fit: Your scripting, automation, and deployment skills transfer directly, but you must now learn to treat trained models as versioned artifacts that degrade over time and need monitoring.
steps_json: ["Complete the MLflow Model Registry tutorial and track a scikit-learn model end to end", "Deploy a containerized model on AWS SageMaker using a free-tier account", "Earn the AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty certification"]

description: Develops, tests, and iterates on structured prompts and prompt frameworks to optimize the performance of large language models for specific business use cases such as customer support, code generation, or content creation. Works closely with product and engineering teams to translate requirements into reliable AI behaviors.
core_tools: OpenAI Playground, Anthropic Claude API, PromptFlow
fit: Your debugging mindset and comfort with iterative testing carry over directly, but you must shift from writing instructions for machines to writing instructions for language models, which behave probabilistically rather than deterministically.
steps_json: ["Complete DeepLearning.AI's free 'ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers' course", "Build three working prompts in OpenAI Playground for code review, bug explanation, and test generation", "Publish a GitHub repo documenting your prompt experiments with version history and output comparisons"]

description: Evaluates and mitigates risks in AI systems including bias, hallucination, adversarial vulnerabilities, and misuse, implementing technical safeguards and red-teaming protocols before and after model deployment. Works within trust-and-safety or responsible AI teams to ensure models meet regulatory and ethical standards.
core_tools: Garak, Microsoft Azure AI Content Safety, Weights & Biases
stat_text: The EU AI Act, which entered into force in August 2024, mandates conformity assessments and technical safety documentation for high-risk AI systems, directly driving demand for AI safety and compliance engineering roles across organizations operating in or selling into the EU.
stat_title: EU AI Act: first regulation on artificial intelligence
fit: Your coding skills transfer directly, but you must shift from building features to actively breaking and auditing AI systems for harm.
steps_json: ["Complete DeepLearning.AI's 'Red Teaming LLMs' short course", "Run Garak against an open-source LLM and document findings in a public GitHub repo", "Earn the Microsoft Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900) certification with focus on responsible AI modules"]

description: Designs conversational interfaces, AI-generated content workflows, and human-AI interaction patterns for products powered by large language models. Conducts user research specific to AI behavior transparency, error states, and trust-building within AI-driven experiences.
core_tools: Figma, OpenAI API, Microsoft Copilot Studio
fit: Your visual design and user research skills transfer directly, but you must shift from designing static screens to designing conversations, AI behavior, and trust-building moments.
steps_json: ["Complete Google UX Design or Nielsen Norman AI UX course", "Build a prototype chatbot using OpenAI API and document the error states", "Redesign one existing app flow in Figma to include AI transparency indicators"]

description: Works at the intersection of Figma and production code, translating visual designs into polished, accessible React components and design systems. description: Designs and prototypes chat-based, voice, and multimodal interfaces for AI assistants, chatbots, and copilot features embedded in enterprise or consumer products. Defines dialogue flows, personality guidelines, and error-handling experiences to make AI interactions feel natural and on-brand.
stat_text: The global conversational AI market is projected to grow from $10.7 billion in 2023 to $29.8 billion by 2028, at a CAGR of 22.6%, driving sustained demand for specialized conversational interface talent.
stat_title: Conversational AI Market — Global Forecast to 2028
search_query: "Conversational UI Designer" OR "Conversational UX Designer" jobs 2025
fit: Your visual layout and user flow skills transfer directly, but you must shift from designing screens to designing spoken and written conversations as the primary interface.

description: Leads end-to-end product design for AI-native applications, defining how machine learning model outputs are surfaced, explained, and acted upon within user interfaces. Collaborates directly with data scientists and ML engineers to translate model capabilities into coherent, ethical, and usable product experiences.

description: Manages and optimizes cloud environments using AI-driven infrastructure-as-code tools, automating provisioning, scaling, and monitoring tasks across AWS, Azure, or GCP. Works daily with Terraform, Ansible, and AI-assisted observability platforms to reduce manual configuration and improve uptime.
fit: Your hands-on server and network management experience transfers directly, but you must shift from maintaining existing systems to writing code that builds and destroys infrastructure automatically.
steps_json: ["Complete HashiCorp Terraform Associate certification", "Deploy a personal AWS environment using Terraform and CloudFormation templates", "Set up a free Datadog trial and build a working monitoring dashboard for that environment"]

description: Deploys, monitors, and maintains the server and network infrastructure that supports AI/ML workloads, including GPU clusters, model-serving endpoints, and vector databases. Ensures high availability and performance tuning for AI application pipelines in production environments.

description: Integrates automated security scanning, compliance checks, and AI-powered threat detection directly into CI/CD pipelines, replacing traditional manual patch management and vulnerability assessment workflows. steps_json: ["Earn the GitHub Actions certification and build a sample CI/CD pipeline", "Complete Snyk's free learning path and scan a personal GitHub repository", "Study for the CompTIA SecurityX (formerly CASP+) certification"]

description: Builds and maintains internal developer platforms (IDPs) that abstract away infrastructure complexity using AI-assisted tooling, enabling software teams to deploy and manage applications without deep ops knowledge. steps_json: ["Complete the CNCF Backstage basics tutorial and build a local developer portal", "Earn the Kubernetes and Cloud Native Associate (KCNA) certification", "Build a Crossplane demo that provisions a database from a Git commit"]

description: Plans and conducts user research to inform product and design decisions. Uses interviews, usability testing, surveys, and behavioral data to surface user needs, validate assumptions, and reduce design risk before and during development.
stat_text: UX Researcher job postings on LinkedIn grew 38% between 2022 and 2025, with AI-native companies increasingly hiring dedicated researchers to study how users interact with AI-generated interfaces and agentic products.
fit: Your design background — understanding user flows, evaluating interfaces, and communicating findings — transfers directly. The shift is from designing solutions to systematically studying the people who use them, then feeding those findings back to design and product teams.
steps_json: ["Complete IDEO Us Human-Centered Design course to formalize research methodology", "Run 5 moderated usability tests using Maze or Lookback on an existing product", "Learn affinity mapping and thematic analysis: document findings from a real research project in Dovetail"]
